text.title: data-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for data-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No data dictionary — every developer defines data elements independently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without shared definitions, integration and reporting produce conflicting numbers that erode trust in all data.

failure-mode.description: Skipping data quality assessment — assuming existing data is correct before migration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Data quality issues discovered during migration cause project delays; assessing early allows remediation to run in parallel with development.

failure-mode.description: No data ownership defined — no one accountable for data accuracy or access.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unowned data degrades over time; ownership assigns the accountability needed to maintain quality rules.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring historical data — modeling current state without considering how data changes over time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Temporal requirements (audit trails, soft deletes, effective-date ranges) discovered after the physical model is built require schema rework.

failure-mode.description: Definition drift between systems: The same entity (Customer, Order, Product) is defined differently across 4-7 systems.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The methodology offers no reconciliation pattern. Force a reconciliation step requiring both sources cited before merge.

failure-mode.description: No lineage column: A data element without lineage (source system → transformations → consumers) is half-documented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents and humans both omit it because the template does not require it. Lineage is mandatory; reject dictionaries lacking it.

failure-mode.description: Static quality scorecard: Six dimensions are point-in-time; quality decays.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without recurring re-assessment, scorecard is outdated within a quarter. Schedule weekly.

failure-mode.description: PII / classification handled as afterthought: "Data Security" is one row at the bottom.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Classification (Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted) drives storage, retention, masking, access. Make it first-class.

failure-mode.description: Volume estimates without growth model: 1yr/3yr columns invite linear extrapolation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents plug numbers without questioning the model. State the model explicitly (linear / exponential / seasonal).

failure-mode.description: No event / state-transition modelling: Entities have lifecycles (Order: created → paid → shipped).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Methodology models attributes but not state machines. Add status enum + transitions table.

failure-mode.description: Conflates relational with non-relational data: ERD assumes relational.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Document stores, event streams, and graph data need different primitives. Agents force everything into entity-attribute-relationship form.

failure-mode.description: Business rules in prose: Rule examples are English sentences, not executable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot verify them mechanically without a DSL (JSON Schema, OPA Rego, dbt tests). Use executable rules.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated columns: Agents invent created_at / updated_at columns that live schema does not have.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pass introspected schema in prompt; reject any column not present without explicit ADD task.

failure-mode.description: Type-coercion blind spots: Agents map varchar(50) to text interchangeably.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In some warehouses (BigQuery STRING vs SQL Server NVARCHAR) the difference matters. Pin types to target dialect.

failure-mode.description: Silent classification downgrade: Agent re-emits attribute and drops Confidential tag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include classification of every existing attribute in prompt; reject diffs lowering classification without ADR.

failure-mode.description: Definition collision: Two systems both define Customer; agent picks one and overwrites.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force reconciliation step requiring both sources cited before merge.

failure-mode.description: PII leakage in sample rows: "Show me 5 example rows" → real emails in prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pre-redact via Presidio or use synthetic samples generated from schema.

failure-mode.description: Volume math fabrication: Agents produce confident 3-year numbers from single multiplier.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require statement of model (linear / exp / seasonal) + assumption inputs; otherwise "needs human estimate".

failure-mode.description: Cardinality misnaming: 1:N vs N:M confusion creates wrong ERDs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constrain agent to derive cardinality from FK + uniqueness constraints in information_schema, never prose.

failure-mode.description: Dropped historical context: Agents rewrite description, losing rationale.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require change-log row appended, never a description replacement.

failure-mode.description: No nullability invariants: Agents flag Required: Y but emit no NOT NULL constraint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bind the dictionary's Required column to migration generator (alembic / liquibase) so they cannot diverge.

failure-mode.description: Inferring meaning from name: is_active differs in CRM vs billing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force lineage + business definition on every Boolean.

failure-mode.description: Token bombs from full schema dumps: 400-table catalog blows context window.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Slice by feature scope; pass only entities the spec touches.

failure-mode.description: Dictionary becomes only source of truth, then rots: Agent updates dictionary but not migration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pair dictionary PRs with migration + dbt tests in same commit, gated by tbls/schemaspy diff.

failure-mode.description: Mermaid ERD limits: Diagrams over 30 entities become unreadable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force per-bounded-context diagrams (one Mermaid file per domain).
